segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: What?

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: You know, give a rationale.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Really?
segments.zh: 真的吗？好吧，那我也改一下。

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: OK, I'll change that too then.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Oh, OK.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: Right.

segments.speaker: TOM
segments.en: So we can use a personal link?
segments.zh: 那我们可以用个人链接？

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Sure.

segments.speaker: JESS
segments.en: Ah right.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ancient philosophy is not just about talking or lecturing, or even reading long, dense books.
segments.zh: 古代哲学不只是空谈、讲课，甚至不是阅读那些又长又难懂的书。实际上，历史上人们一直用它来解决问题、取得最伟大的成就。具体来说，我指的是斯多葛主义，在我看来，这是所有哲学中最实用的，因此也最吸引人。斯多葛主义由季蒂昂的芝诺于公元前3世纪初在古希腊创立，但像埃皮克提图、加图、塞内加和马可·奥勒留这样的人都在践行它。令人惊讶的是，尽管最著名的斯多葛主义者从未为出版写过任何东西，我们仍然能接触到这些思想。加图肯定没有。马可·奥勒留从未打算让他的《沉思录》成为公开作品，只是个人笔记。塞内加的信件，嗯，就是信件，而埃皮克提图的思想则是通过一个记笔记的学生流传下来的。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, it is something people have used throughout history – to solve their problems and to achieve their greatest triumphs.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Specifically, I am referring to Stoicism, which, in my opinion, is the most practical of all philosophies and therefore the most appealing.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Stoicism was founded in Ancient Greece by Zeno of Citium in the early 3rd century BC, but was practised by the likes of Epictetus, Cato, Seneca and Marcus Aurelius.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Amazingly, we still have access to these ideas, despite the fact that the most famous Stoics never wrote anything down for publication.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Cato definitely didn't.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Marcus Aurelius never intended his Meditations to be anything but personal.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Seneca's letters were, well, letters and Epictetus' thoughts come to us by way of a note-taking student.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Stoic principles were based on the idea that its followers could have an unshakable happiness in this life and the key to achieving this was virtue.
segments.zh: 斯多葛原则基于这样的理念：其追随者可以在今生获得不可动摇的幸福，而实现这一点的关键是美德。而通往美德的道路在于理解：像愤怒和嫉妒这样的破坏性情绪是受我们有意识控制的——它们不必控制我们，因为我们可以学会控制它们。用埃皮克提图的话说：“外部事件我无法控制，但我可以控制我对它们所做的选择。”

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The road to virtue, in turn, lay in understanding that destructive emotions, like anger and jealousy, are under our conscious control – they don't have to control us, because we can learn to control them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the words of Epictetus: "external events I cannot control, but the choices I make with regard to them, I do control".

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The modern day philosopher and writer Nassim Nicholas Taleb defines a Stoic as someone who has a different perspective on experiences which most of us would see as wholly negative; a Stoic "transforms fear into caution, pain into transformation, mistakes into initiation and desire into undertaking".
segments.zh: 现代哲学家兼作家纳西姆·尼古拉斯·塔勒布将斯多葛主义者定义为：对大多数人所认为的完全负面的经历持有不同视角的人；斯多葛主义者“将恐惧转化为谨慎，将痛苦转化为转变，将错误转化为开端，将欲望转化为事业”。以这个定义作为模型，我们可以看到，在过去的几个世纪里，斯多葛主义在更近的历史中被国王、总统、艺术家、作家和企业家所践行。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Using this definition as a model, we can see that throughout the centuries Stoicism has been practised in more recent history by kings, presidents, artists, writers and entrepreneurs.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The founding fathers of the United States were inspired by the philosophy.
segments.zh: 美国的开国元勋们受到了这种哲学的启发。乔治·华盛顿在17岁时通过邻居接触到了斯多葛主义，后来还上演了一部基于加图生平的话剧来激励他的士兵。托马斯·杰斐逊在床边放了一本塞内加的作品。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: George Washington was introduced to Stoicism by his neighbours at age seventeen, and later, put on a play based on the life of Cato to inspire his men.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Thomas Jefferson kept a copy of Seneca beside his bed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Writers and artists have also been inspired by the stoics.
segments.zh: 作家和艺术家也受到了斯多葛主义者的启发。欧仁·德拉克罗瓦，这位著名的法国浪漫主义艺术家（以其画作《自由引导人民》最为人熟知），是一位热忱的斯多葛主义者，将其称为他的“慰藉宗教”。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Eugène Delacroix, the renowned French Romantic artist (known best for his painting Liberty Leading the People) was an ardent Stoic, referring to it as his "consoling religion".

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The economist Adam Smith's theories on capitalism were significantly influenced by the Stoicism that he studied as a schoolboy, under a teacher who had translated Marcus Aurelius' works.
segments.zh: 经济学家亚当·斯密的资本主义理论深受他学生时代所学的斯多葛主义的影响，当时他的老师曾翻译过马可·奥勒留的作品。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Today's political leaders are no different, with many finding their inspiration from the ancient texts.
segments.zh: 今天的政治领袖也不例外，许多人从古代文本中寻找灵感。美国前总统比尔·克林顿每年都读马可·奥勒留的作品，许多人将前总统奥巴马的冷静领导风格与加图相提并论。中国前总理温家宝声称《沉思录》是他旅行时随身携带的两本书之一，并且他一生中读过一百多遍。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Former US president Bill Clinton reads Marcus Aurelius every single year, and many have compared former President Obama's calm leadership style to that of Cato.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Wen Jiabao, the former prime minister of China, claims that Meditations is one of two books he travels with and that he has read it more than one hundred times over the course of his life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Stoicism had a profound influence on Albert Ellis, who invented Cognitive Behaviour Therapy, which is used to help people manage their problems by changing the way that they think and behave.
segments.zh: 斯多葛主义对阿尔伯特·埃利斯产生了深远的影响，他发明了认知行为疗法，该疗法通过改变人们的思维和行为方式来帮助他们处理问题。它最常用于治疗抑郁症。其理念是，我们可以通过用逻辑挑战那些导致错误思维、症状和行为的不合理信念，从而掌控自己的生活。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's most commonly used to treat depression.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The idea is that we can take control of our lives by challenging the irrational beliefs that create our faulty thinking, symptoms and behaviours by using logic instead.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Stoicism has also become popular in the world of business.
segments.zh: 斯多葛主义在商界也变得流行起来。斯多葛原则可以培养克服挫折所需的韧性和心态，因为斯多葛学派教导人们将障碍转化为机遇。这是每个企业家都需要学习的一课。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Stoic principles can build the resilience and state of mind required to overcome setbacks because Stoics teach turning obstacles into opportunity.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A lesson every business entrepreneur needs to learn.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I would argue that studying Stoicism is as relevant today as it was 2,000 years ago, thanks to its brilliant insights into how to lead a good life.
segments.zh: 我认为，研究斯多葛主义在今天和两千年前一样有意义，因为它对如何过上好生活有着深刻的见解。这种思想的核心是一种非常简单的生活方式——控制你能控制的，接受你不能控制的。这听起来容易做起来难，需要大量的练习——可能需要一生的时间来掌握。斯多葛学派还认为，美好幸福生活最重要的基础不是金钱、名誉、权力或快乐，而是拥有自律和有原则的品格——这一点似乎引起了许多现代人的共鸣。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: At the very root of the thinking, there is a very simple way of living – control what you can and accept what you can't.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is not as easy as it sounds and will require considerable practice – it can take a lifetime to master.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Stoics also believed the most important foundation for a good and happy life is not money, fame, power or pleasure, but having a disciplined and principled character – something which seems to resonate with many people today.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: simple way of living – control what you can and accept what you can't.
segments.zh: 简单的生活方式——控制你能控制的，接受你不能控制的。这听起来容易做起来难，需要大量的练习——可能需要一生的时间来掌握。斯多葛学派还认为，美好幸福生活最重要的基础不是金钱、名誉、权力或快乐，而是拥有自律和有原则的品格——这一点似乎引起了许多现代人的共鸣。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is not as easy as it sounds and will require considerable practice – it can take a lifetime to master.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Stoics also believed the most important foundation for a good and happy life is not money, fame, power or pleasure, but having a disciplined and principled character – something which seems to resonate with many people today.

items.prompt: Stoicism is still relevant today because of its ______ appeal.

pairs.note: 题干说'still relevant today'，原文说'used throughout history'，意思相同，都是指持续有用。
题干中的'appeal'是名词，原文中'appealing'是形容词，词根相同，直接对应。
题干答案词'practical'在原文中直接出现，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Stoicism是'most practical'，因此'practical'是答案。题干中的'relevant today'对应原文的'used throughout history'，'appeal'对应'appealing'。

items.prompt: The Stoics' ideas are surprisingly well known, despite not being intended for ______.
Epictetus said that external events cannot be controlled but the ______ people make in response can be controlled.

pairs.q: external events cannot be controlled but the ______ people make in response can be controlled
pairs.p: the choices I make with regard to them, I do control
pairs.note: 原文直接对应题干，'choices'是答案词，'make'与题干'people make'一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句是Epictetus的直接引语，与题干几乎逐字对应，只需找到'choices'即可。

items.prompt: A Stoic is someone who has a different view on experiences which others would consider as ______.

pairs.note: view 和 perspective 是同义词，都表示看法、观点。

pairs.p: most of us would see as
pairs.note: 题干中的 others 对应原文的 most of us，consider as 对应 see as，都是表示‘认为’的短语。
答案 negative 直接出现在原文中，只是多了副词 wholly 修饰。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接给出了 Stoic 的定义，其中‘different perspective’对应题干‘different view’，‘most of us would see as’对应‘others would consider as’，而答案‘negative’就在‘wholly negative’中。

items.prompt: George Washington organised a ______ about Cato to motivate his men.

pairs.note: organised 和 put on 都表示‘组织、上演’的意思

pairs.p: a play based on the life of Cato
pairs.note: 原文直接给出 play，题干空格处应填 play

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到‘put on a play based on the life of Cato’，其中‘put on’对应题干‘organised’，‘a play’直接对应空格，因此答案就是 play。

items.prompt: Adam Smith's ideas on ______ were influenced by Stoicism.

pairs.note: ideas 和 theories 是同义替换，都表示观点、理论。
原文多了 significantly，但核心动词相同，可直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 Adam Smith 关于什么的观点受斯多葛主义影响，定位句直接说他的资本主义理论受斯多葛主义影响，所以答案就是 capitalism。

items.prompt: the treatment for ______ is based on ideas from Stoicism

pairs.note: treatment for 和 used to treat 都表示'用于治疗'，是同义替换。

pairs.p: Stoicism had a profound influence on
pairs.note: based on ideas from 和 had a profound influence on 都表示'受其思想影响'，是近义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 Cognitive Behaviour Therapy 受斯多葛主义影响，并常用于治疗 depression，因此答案就是 depression。

items.prompt: people learn to base their thinking on ______

pairs.note: 题干说'基于...思考'，原文说'用逻辑代替'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句最后说'by using logic instead'，其中'using logic'对应题干'base their thinking on logic'，所以答案填logic。

items.prompt: In business, people benefit from Stoicism by identifying obstacles as ______

pairs.note: 题干用'identifying...as'，原文用'turning...into'，意思都是'把障碍视为/转化为'
答案词直接出现在原文中，完全一致

paraphrase.explain: 定位句明确提到'Stoics teach turning obstacles into opportunity'，其中'turning obstacles into'对应题干'identifying obstacles as'，'opportunity'就是答案。

items.prompt: It requires a lot of ______ but Stoicism can help people to lead a good life.

pairs.note: 'requires a lot of' 被替换为 'require considerable'，都表示需要很多。
答案 'practice' 在原文中直接出现，完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'will require considerable practice'，其中 'require considerable' 对应题干 'requires a lot of'，'practice' 就是答案。
